summary: Verified iteration-37 email status tracking fix. Backend stats endpoint /api/admin/email-queue/stats returns proper per-status counts for queue+logs (PASS). Existing email_logs no longer have stale 'queued' entries — historical backfill worked (PASS). send_templated_email correctly flips status from 'sending' to 'sent'/'failed' (PASS, visible in UI as all registration_submitted/membership_approved logs show 'sent'). HOWEVER — the fix is INCOMPLETE: the POST /api/admin/email/send endpoint (used by the Admin > Email System > 'Compose Email' UI) still inserts an email_log row with status='queued' and NEVER updates it after the SMTP send completes. Both the backend test email and the UI-triggered test email are stuck on 'queued' status — the exact bug this iteration was supposed to fix.

critical.issue: Handler in server.py lines 2473-2495 inserts email_logs row with status='queued', then calls background_tasks.add_task(send_email_smtp, ...) but never updates the log to 'sent' or 'failed' after the SMTP call finishes. This is EXACTLY the bug that was supposed to be fixed. The fix was only applied to send_templated_email() (lines 976-1013), not to the admin_send_email() route. Verified: TEST_status_tracking_iter37 (backend test) and TEST_UI_iter37_email_status (UI test) both remain 'queued' in /api/admin/email/logs after ~10s wait.

minor.issue: Handler (line 1608) writes email_logs with status='sent' BEFORE the background SMTP task runs — so if SMTP fails, the log will falsely show 'sent'. Same anti-pattern as the fixed one but in reverse. Should be updated to use the same try/except pattern as send_templated_email.

integration_issues.issue: After sending, the email log row in the Email Logs table appears with status badge 'queued' (yellow pending badge) and never transitions to 'sent' or 'failed'. Root cause is the backend endpoint POST /api/admin/email/send (see backend_issues.critical).

action_items: FIX HIGH: Update POST /api/admin/email/send handler in /app/backend/server.py (around line 2473) to use the same try/except pattern as send_templated_email — insert log with status='sending', then update to 'sent'/'failed' after send_email_smtp returns. Because it uses background_tasks, wrap the send+update in a helper coroutine and enqueue that helper (not the raw send_email_smtp).
OPTIONAL: Fix POST /api/admin/members/{id}/send-email (line 1608) which pre-marks the log as 'sent' before the background task runs (incorrect for SMTP failures).

Optional backfill (already done for logs — do it again after applying the fix above to catch new stuck rows): db.email_logs.updateMany({status: 'queued'}, {$set: {status: 'sent'}}) — but only after the endpoint is fixed.
critical_code_review_comments: The 'fix' logic in send_templated_email (line 976) is correct — status transitions from 'sending' to 'sent'/'failed'. However, the same pattern must be applied everywhere db.email_logs gets an initial insert with a non-terminal status. Currently the admin_send_email route inserts 'queued' and never touches the row again.

server.py is >5300 lines. Consider splitting email/notification logic into a dedicated module (backend/services/email.py) to avoid this class of bug where fixes are applied inconsistently across duplicated code paths.

queue_bulk_emails (line 3941) inserts a summary email_logs row with status='queued'. Since these are audit records for a *batch*, they are never intended to move to 'sent' — but they still show up in the logs stats bucket as 'queued'. Consider using a distinct status like 'batch_queued' to avoid confusing UI reporting.

success_rate.backend: 75% (3/4 tests pass — 1 fails due to real bug)
success_rate.frontend: 50% (page loads + compose works, but status stays 'queued')

seed_data_creation: Inserted 2 test rows into email_logs via /api/admin/email/send with subject 'TEST_status_tracking_iter37' and 'TEST_UI_iter37_email_status' — both stuck on 'queued' status (evidence of the bug). Main agent may want to delete these after fix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The core issue: send_templated_email is fixed but admin_send_email route (POST /api/admin/email/send) is NOT. The frontend 'Compose Email' button in Admin > Email System uses the buggy route directly (see EmailAdmin.jsx line 32). After E1 applies the fix, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_email_queue_status.py — all 4 tests should pass. Also verify UI: send a compose email and confirm the row shows 'sent' or 'failed' within 5-10s (not 'queued').
rca of the issue: Bug RCA — Iteration 37: E1 fixed the status-tracking gap in send_templated_email() (lines 976-1013) by wrapping the SMTP send in try/except and updating email_logs.status from 'sending' -> 'sent' or 'failed'. However, the identical anti-pattern still exists in the admin_send_email() route handler (server.py lines 2473-2495): it inserts email_logs with status='queued' and then calls background_tasks.add_task(send_email_smtp, ...) — with no follow-up status update. The stale 'queued' rows in the DB from before were backfilled to 'sent' (verified — 0 remaining), but the fix does NOT prevent NEW rows from getting stuck on 'queued'. Reproduction: (1) Login to /admin/email as super admin, (2) Click 'Compose Email', (3) Choose 'Custom Recipients', enter any email, subject, body, click Send, (4) Row appears in Email Logs table with status='queued' and stays there forever. Mitigation: Refactor admin_send_email to follow the same pattern used in send_templated_email — insert with a non-terminal status ('sending'), enqueue a wrapper coroutine that calls send_email_smtp AND updates the log row's status based on the return value / exception.
